attributes.name: VBScript WMI Security Product Enumeration
attributes.description: Detects cscript.exe or wscript.exe executing VBScript files that perform WMI queries against SecurityCenter2 or Windows Defender namespaces to enumerate installed antivirus products. This is a signature reconnaissance technique used by APT42 TAMECAT during initial access to profile endpoint security posture before deploying the fileless backdoor.
attributes.query: process where event.type == "start" and process.name in ("cscript.exe", "wscript.exe") and process.args regex~ """.*\.(vbs|vbe|wsf)$"""
author: F0RT1KA Defense Guidance Builder
false_positives: Enterprise IT management scripts that query AV status via WMI

attributes.name: PowerShell Encoded Command from Conhost Headless Execution
attributes.description: Detects the TAMECAT-specific execution pattern where conhost.exe is launched with the --headless flag to spawn powershell.exe with -EncodedCommand. This technique is used by APT42 to execute fileless PowerShell payloads while evading console-based detection mechanisms. The headless conhost provides a hidden execution context.
attributes.query: process where event.type == "start" and process.name == "conhost.exe" and process.args == "--headless" and process.args in ("powershell.exe", "pwsh.exe", "cmd.exe")
author: F0RT1KA Defense Guidance Builder
false_positives: Very low -- headless conhost spawning PowerShell is extremely rare in legitimate usage

attributes.name: PowerShell Encoded Command with Large Payload
attributes.description: Detects PowerShell execution with -EncodedCommand parameter containing a large base64-encoded payload (>200 characters), launched from suspicious parent processes. Large encoded commands are characteristic of fileless malware loaders including TAMECAT that encode their entire payload for in-memory execution.
attributes.query: process where event.type == "start" and process.name == "powershell.exe" and process.args in ("-EncodedCommand", "-enc", "-ec") and process.parent.name in ("conhost.exe", "cmd.exe", "cscript.exe", "wscript.exe", "mshta.exe", "explorer.exe", "wmiprvse.exe")
author: F0RT1KA Defense Guidance Builder
false_positives: Some enterprise deployment tools use encoded PowerShell commands

attributes.name: UserInitMprLogonScript Registry Persistence
attributes.description: Detects modification of the UserInitMprLogonScript registry value under HKCU\Environment. This is a rare persistence mechanism heavily favored by Iranian APT groups including APT42/Magic Hound. The value specifies a script that executes during user logon before the shell loads, making it difficult to detect with standard startup monitoring.
attributes.query: registry where event.type in ("change", "creation") and registry.path regex~ """.*\\Environment\\UserInitMprLogonScript"""
author: F0RT1KA Defense Guidance Builder
false_positives: Enterprise login scripts deployed via GPO (verify with IT team)

attributes.name: Registry Run Key Persistence Targeting Script Interpreter
attributes.description: Detects creation of Registry Run key values that invoke script interpreters (cscript.exe, wscript.exe, powershell.exe, mshta.exe) with script file arguments. APT42 creates a Run key named 'Renovation' pointing to cscript.exe with a VBScript payload for persistence across reboots.
attributes.query: registry where event.type in ("change", "creation") and registry.path regex~ """.*\\CurrentVersion\\Run\\.*""" and registry.data.strings regex~ """(?i).*(cscript|wscript|mshta|powershell).*\.(vbs|vbe|js|jse|wsf|ps1).*"""
author: F0RT1KA Defense Guidance Builder
false_positives: Legitimate software using Run keys for startup VBScript execution

attributes.name: Browser Credential Database Access by Non-Browser Process
attributes.description: Detects access to Chromium-based browser credential databases (Login Data, Cookies, Web Data) by processes other than the browser itself. APT42 copies these SQLite databases for offline credential extraction using DPAPI decryption. This covers Chrome, Edge, Brave, Opera, and Vivaldi credential stores.
attributes.query: file where event.type in ("access", "creation") and file.name in ("Login Data", "Cookies", "Web Data", "Local State") and file.path regex~ """.*\\(Google\\Chrome|Microsoft\\Edge|BraveSoftware\\Brave-Browser)\\User Data\\.*""" and not process.name in ("chrome.exe", "msedge.exe", "brave.exe", "opera.exe", "MsMpEng.exe", "SearchProtocolHost.exe")
author: F0RT1KA Defense Guidance Builder
false_positives: Antivirus performing real-time scanning of browser data

attributes.name: Browser Remote Debugging Port Activation
attributes.description: Detects Chromium-based browsers launched with --remote-debugging-port flag. Attackers use this to connect to the Chrome DevTools Protocol (CDP) to extract cookies, session tokens, and credentials from active browser sessions without needing to decrypt DPAPI-protected data.
attributes.query: process where event.type == "start" and process.name in ("msedge.exe", "chrome.exe", "brave.exe") and process.args : "--remote-debugging-port=*"
author: F0RT1KA Defense Guidance Builder
false_positives: Web developers using browser debugging tools
CI/CD pipelines running browser-based tests (Selenium, Puppeteer, Playwright)

attributes.name: Telegram API Communication from Non-Telegram Application
attributes.description: Detects DNS resolution or network connections to api.telegram.org from processes that are not the official Telegram desktop client. APT42 uses the Telegram Bot API as a C2 channel and data exfiltration mechanism, sending stolen credentials and system information to attacker-controlled Telegram channels.
attributes.query: dns where dns.question.name == "api.telegram.org" and not process.name in ("Telegram.exe", "TelegramDesktop.exe", "Updater.exe") and not process.executable regex~ """.*\\Telegram Desktop\\.*"""
author: F0RT1KA Defense Guidance Builder
false_positives: Custom Telegram bots running on the workstation (should be on servers)
Browser navigating to Telegram web interface

attributes.name: Malicious LNK File Creation Targeting Script Interpreter
attributes.description: Detects creation of .lnk shortcut files by processes other than Explorer or standard Windows Shell operations. Malicious LNK files are a primary APT42 delivery mechanism, disguised as documents but targeting cscript.exe or wscript.exe to execute VBScript downloaders.
attributes.query: file where event.type == "creation" and file.extension == "lnk" and not process.name in ("explorer.exe", "SearchProtocolHost.exe", "msiexec.exe", "setup.exe")
author: F0RT1KA Defense Guidance Builder
false_positives: Group Policy creating shortcuts during logon

attributes.name: APT42 TAMECAT Kill Chain - Multi-Stage Correlation
attributes.description: Correlates multiple APT42 TAMECAT kill chain indicators within a time window: VBScript execution followed by encoded PowerShell followed by registry persistence modifications. Triggers when 2 or more distinct technique stages are observed in sequence on the same host within 30 minutes.
attributes.query: sequence by host.id with maxspan=30m [process where event.type == "start" and process.name in ("cscript.exe", "wscript.exe") and process.args regex~ """.*\.vbs$"""] [process where event.type == "start" and process.name == "powershell.exe" and process.args in ("-EncodedCommand", "-enc", "-ec")] [registry where event.type in ("change", "creation") and registry.path regex~ """.*\\(CurrentVersion\\Run\\.*|Environment\\UserInitMprLogonScript)"""]
author: F0RT1KA Defense Guidance Builder
false_positives: Extremely low -- this sequence of VBScript execution, encoded PowerShell, and registry persistence within 30 minutes is highly indicative of APT activity
